title: Internal Error in V3DfgBreakCycles: Wrong result width
body:
[Input here](https://gist.github.com/widlarizer/12f67d2966b95615865b882acfceb79d)
Repro: `verilator --lint-only mul-synth-bad-verilator.v`
Prints:
```
%Error: Internal Error: mul-synth-bad-verilator.v:178:15: ../V3DfgBreakCycles.cpp:183: Wrong result width
  178 |   wire [31:0] _0076_;
      |               ^~~~~~
```
It's a result of techmapping this Verilog with Yosys:
```v
module Solution
(
    input wire clk,
    input wire reset,
    output wire i_ready,
    input wire i_valid,
    input wire [16-1:0] i_payload_a,
    input wire [16-1:0] i_payload_b,
    output wire [32-1:0] o_payload,
    output wire o_valid
);
    assign o_valid = i_valid;
    assign i_ready = 1'b1;
    assign o_payload = i_payload_a * i_payload_b;
endmodule
```
The problem goes away when I reduce the widths of the summands and result, so I can't really reduce the input.
`Verilator 5.040 2025-08-30 rev v5.040`
Sorry I don't have a newer `verilator` on hand. Tested on Linux, nixOS unstable
